preamble: The transformation of the voiced bilabial plosive /b/ into the voiceless bilabial fricative /ɸ/ is a documented sound change in historical linguistics. This shift exemplifies a combination of lenition, where a stop consonant weakens to a fricative, and devoicing, where the vocal cords cease vibration during articulation. Such changes often occur in environments that favor reduced articulatory effort.
phoneticEffects: Lenition, Spirantization, Devoicing, Opening of articulation

examples.note: A noninitial Proto-language *b underwent a phonemic split to /ɸ/ and /v/.
A noninitial Proto-language *b underwent a phonemic split to /ɸ/ and /v/.
